{{Quote|These ill-tempered trash-diggers have been traced to Escabar, but they appeared to have stowed away on some early vessel from that planet and propagated themselves across the known worlds.|An individual, on the borcatu|<ref name="Creatures" />}}
'''Escabar''' was a [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="Creatures" /> located in the [[Escabar system/Legends|Escabar system]], a part of the [[Freestanding subsectors/Legends|freestanding subsectors]] of the [[Expansion Region/Legends|Expansion Region]].<ref name="EAOC" /> It featured the regions [[Khrassh]] and [[Serhan]], the latter of which had [[Desert/Legends|deserts]] in its northeastern areas. Despite the fierce [[Sandstorm/Legends|sandstorms]] of those territories, the small, [[Limb/Legends|quadripedal]] [[Diet/Legends|scavenger]] creatures known as the [[Borcatu/Legends|borcatu]] evolved there, adapting for digging burrows and cracking open shells.<ref name="Creatures" />

At some point, interstellar [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceports]] were built and opened on Escabar. When [[Merchant/Legends|traders]] from Khrassh crossed the Serhan deserts on their way to the ports, several borcatu climbed aboard the departing [[Starship/Legends|starships]] unnoticed and left the planet. Over [[Time/Legends|time]], populations of the creatures spread across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], with the [[Species/Legends|species]] eventually being traced back to their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]]. 150 [[Standard year/Legends|standard years]] after Escabar became a member of the [[galactic economy]], an [[Unidentified individual (borcatu)|individual]] described the origin of the borcatu on Escabar and the creatures' spread from the planet.<ref name="Creatures" /> Between [[12 ABY/Legends|12 ABY]] and [[13 ABY/Legends|13 ABY]], Escabar was a part of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].<ref name="EGTW" />

==Behind the scenes==
Escabar was first mentioned in the [[1994]] sourcebook ''[[Creatures of the Galaxy]]'', specifically in the short companion narrative "[[A Widespread Scavenger]]" that was authored by [[Phil Brucato]].<ref name="Creatures" /> The book was published by [[West End Games]] for use with ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]''.<ref name="Creatures" /> The [[2009]] reference book ''[[The Essential Atlas]]'' placed the Escabar [[Star system/Legends|system]], and therefore the planet itself, in grid square I-8.<ref name="TEA" />
